persona_pain: Deals dying in silence while champions go dark and pipeline looks fine until it doesn't
persona_blurb: You carry a number. Your whole job depends on deals staying alive and champions staying warm. Most CRM data tells you what happened last month — these plays tell you what to do today about the deal that's going quiet. You need fast inbound follow-up, multi-threading on booked meetings, and reactivation plays for pipeline that's been silent too long.

recipes.name: Auto-connect on meeting or signup
recipes.outcome: Draft a same-day LinkedIn touch for approval when prospects book or submit, before the moment cools.
recipes.needs: HubSpot or FirstTouch-accessible inbound list/import required

recipes.name: Social engager flow — leadership's audience
recipes.outcome: Reach people engaging with company or leadership content before they go cold.
recipes.needs: No HubSpot required; use company/leadership, competitor founder, or influencer profile engagement; HubSpot optional for qualification/routing; profile views unavailable

recipes.name: Website visitor play
recipes.outcome: Turn pricing/demo/product-page visits into LinkedIn touches while intent is active; if no visitor signal source exists, choose the AE AI SDR recipe as a separate prospecting motion instead.
recipes.needs: HubSpot tracking or RB2B/list source required; if no visitor signal source exists, use the persona AI SDR recipe as a separate prospecting motion

recipes.name: AE AI SDR — daily approval queue
recipes.outcome: View a HubSpot contact/company list or discover a new ICP list, enrich each prospect, and draft up to 10/day free or 20/day Sales Nav/Premium AE-owned LinkedIn touches for approval.
recipes.needs: No HubSpot required with ICP brief + FirstTouch Discover Contacts; HubSpot list optional

recipes.name: Social campaigns — territory and deal expansion
recipes.outcome: Build a narrow AE push for a territory, event, customer expansion, or stalled-pipeline segment. Default to row-level dynamic approvals like AI SDR; use static flow-level approval only when RevOps/founder approves the exact audience and templates.
recipes.needs: No HubSpot required for pure ICP/imported/Discover segments; HubSpot required for territory/deal/customer CRM segments; row-level approval by default

recipes.name: Stalled deal reactivation — 60-day open-deal workflow
recipes.outcome: Find contacts associated to open deals that are not Closed Won/Lost and have had no engagement for 60+ days, then build an owner-approved LinkedIn reactivation queue. Treat recurring workflow setup as a RevOps/admin step unless already configured.
recipes.needs: HubSpot required; RevOps/admin may be needed only for recurring workflow setup

recipes.name: Meeting-booked stakeholder follow-up
recipes.outcome: When a meeting-booked HubSpot list/workflow or accessible source already exists, draft LinkedIn touches to known decision-makers so the deal is not single-threaded; otherwise ask RevOps for one-time setup first.
recipes.needs: HubSpot required; needs a contact-based list/workflow or accessible meeting-booked source; ask RevOps if the AE cannot configure CRM workflows

recipes.name: Closed-lost reengagement
recipes.outcome: Re-engage historical closed-lost accounts with a fresh, approved LinkedIn touch when the user explicitly chooses a win-back motion; keep this separate from stalled open-deal reactivation.
recipes.needs: HubSpot required; separate from stalled open-deal workflow
